approach: RENAME (freeze-preserving reconcile): cyberfleet Tuner persona -> Mechanic everywhere. skill dir plugins/cyberfleet/skills/tuner/ -> mechanic/; spec folder .agents/specs/cyberfleet-plugin/tuning/ -> mechanic/ and tuning.feature -> mechanic.feature; website doc + nav slug; cross-refs in Crimp/Tavern/recruitment. gherkin-cli keys scenarios by title so the retitle reads remove+add, but the Given/When/Then oracle is unchanged for the rename-only scenarios -> freeze-preserving, self-clears, stays @frozen.

VOICE: anchor Mechanic to NieR 'Jackass' (Resistance workshop engineer, bench tinkerer, chip-in/chip-out), keep the plug-in-chip identity; follow the Operator convention (plain role-name + named NieR voice).

CHARTER build+tune: Mechanic owns the automaton artifact end-to-end. An automaton = human-facing gateway skill (not a subagent). NEW automaton -> route to define-skill (ADDITIVE self-clear). Stays a thin in-session dispatcher: opens no CR, invokes no gate, spawns nothing.

RE-OPEN (routing fix, ratified under conductor leash): re-chip of governance/loadout routed to define-agent/improve-agent-definition (subagent engines) -> corrected to define-skill/improve-skill (automatons are skills). Model/effort still -> manage-model-runners; leash -> autonomy rubric.

RE-OPEN (boundary charter change, ratified under conductor leash): drop 'target automaton does not exist yet -> hand to Crimp to author'; Mechanic now builds it via define-skill. Twin trigger-side narrowing: author-from-scratch boundary now excludes automatons (a plain workflow skill still defers to define-skill directly). Crimp stays recruit-existing; Operator stays deploy/spawn; define-agent stays subagents-only.

note: the frozen tuning.feature scenario 'a governance or loadout re-chip routes to the agent-definition engines' pinned the engine as define-agent/improve-agent-definition, which author *subagents* (judges/producers), not automatons. An automaton is a human-facing gateway *skill*, so the correct engine is define-skill/improve-skill. Corrected the scenario oracle + dimension names; ratified as a re-open under conductor leash. Not a weakening of any acceptance guarantee beyond the intended engine correction.

why: mechanic.feature (renamed from tuning.feature): 11 freeze-preserving-renames (trigger-outline with existing rows' should_trigger values unchanged, recruit/deploy/never-deploy boundaries, fast-path, bare-menu, model/effort, leash, advise-model, hot-swap, mixed-split, defer-clean rubric — all Given/When/Then oracles + rubric dimensions/thresholds intact); 3 ADDITIVE (new 'building a brand-new automaton routes to define-skill' scenario + 2 new trigger yes-rows) self-clear; 3 RE-OPENs ratified under conductor leash (per brief, Council-pre-decided charter): (#9) re-chip engine define-agent/improve-agent-definition -> define-skill/improve-skill correction; (#13) build-boundary polarity flip 'only reconfigures, defers authoring to Crimp' -> 'builds via define-skill'; (#4) author-from-scratch trigger boundary narrowed to exclude automatons (plain workflow skill still -> define-skill). Independent cold judge ALIGNED true, confirming the rename/additive split and flagging #4 as the twin re-open (adopted). Floor: none (no acceptance scenario deleted or weakened outside the intended charter widening). Stays @frozen. Same freeze-preserving reference-rename applied to peer-persona mentions in recruitment.feature and tavern.feature (Crimp/Tavern behaviors unchanged).

plugins/cyberfleet/skills/mechanic/SKILL.md conforms to the frozen mechanic.feature: name mechanic, Jackass workshop-engineer voice with plug-in-chip identity; build-a-new-automaton -> define-skill; re-chip governance/loadout -> define-skill/improve-skill (explicitly NOT define-agent/improve-agent-definition, which are subagent-only); model/effort -> manage-model-runners; leash -> autonomy rubric; advises model switch, never silent; confirms hot-swap naming both units; builds not-yet-existing automatons rather than deferring authoring to Crimp; defers recruit-published to Crimp and deploy-ship to Operator; thin in-session dispatcher (no CR/gate/spawn). README + website + Crimp/Tavern cross-refs migrated consistently. pnpm verify green (20/20; gherkin parse + align-spec no drift + no leaks). Floor: none. Stays @frozen.

note: No changeset added: the touched paths (plugins/cyberfleet, .agents/specs, apps/website) are outside the changeset-tracked workspace globs (packages/*, apps/* minus ignored 'website') — no publishable npm package changed, consistent with prior plugin-persona CRs (split-gateway-personas, fleet-crew-personas) which added none. The concept-index by-concept table in spec.md was hand-edited to keep mechanic/ (was tuning/) in sync (alphabetical order preserved: mechanic before recruitment).
